title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS backed by $85M+ in funding, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale the operational backbone of its GTM engine. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in RevOps/Sales Ops within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and the ability to align Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR across globally distributed teams including the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Excellent match across all key dimensions: 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps experience exceeds 7+ requirement, Fintech (cloud cost management) aligns with strong fit verticals, Salesforce expertise directly applicable, and scaling experience matches $30M+ ARR scope. Tel Aviv HQ location is ideal fit.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of Revenue Operations role and thought of you immediately—building a RevOps function from the ground up at a well-funded, fast-scaling SaaS company with a Tel Aviv base is your wheelhouse. Your 15+ years scaling B2B GTM engines and deep Salesforce chops would be critical in aligning their distributed teams across Tel Aviv and New York. Would be worth a quick conversation to explore if this timing works.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong seeks a RevOps Manager to redesign territory models and processes for global scalability. You'll partner with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory composition, analyze performance and market data for segmentation, and support GTM planning cycles with coverage recommendations. Requires deep understanding of B2B SaaS GTM models, strong analytics skills (Excel, SQL, BI tools like Tableau/Retool), and CRM proficiency, especially Salesforce. Salary range $149,000–$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment with Omri's core RevOps expertise in B2B SaaS GTM strategy, territory optimization, and pipeline forecasting. Gong's focus on sales analytics and CRM-driven operations directly matches his 15+ years of revenue operations leadership.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I've spent 15+ years optimizing revenue operations for scaling B2B SaaS companies—including territory modeling, pipeline forecasting, and GTM process redesign at companies similar to Gong's stage. Your RevOps Manager role combining territory strategy with Salesforce analytics is exactly the kind of impactful work I'm targeting, and I'd welcome a conversation about how I can help scale Gong's global operations.

title: Revenue Operations (RevOps) Manager
description: Passage Health is hiring its first dedicated RevOps leader to serve as a core member of GTM leadership and architect of data, insights, and operational excellence across Sales and Marketing. You'll work directly with the CEO and GTM leadership to define key metrics, uncover opportunities, and shape strategy. Requires 3+ years in RevOps/BizOps at early-stage startups or consulting, ideally in SaaS or health tech, plus a strong analytical and systems mindset and experience building GTM systems and processes.

Primary concern: Passage Health appears early-stage/seed, and the role explicitly targets 3+ years experience (Omri has 15+), which may indicate a compensation/scope mismatch or that they're underfunded for a senior hire.
ai_opener: I noticed Passage Health is building out its first dedicated RevOps function—exactly the inflection point where a seasoned GTM architect can compound value fastest. With 15 years scaling revenue systems across B2B SaaS, I've built the playbooks for pipeline forecasting, metrics-driven GTM strategy, and Sales/Marketing alignment that typically take early-stage teams months to discover. I'd be curious whether you're open to a RevOps leader who can shape both the immediate operational excellence and the long-term revenue infrastructure.

Responsibilities include hands-on Salesforce administration (object design, workflow automation, reporting, data governance) or HubSpot lifecycle/lead scoring management, building executive dashboards covering pipeline health, conversion, forecasting and churn/expansion.
